author: Lifeline
text:
Scoped but not started (deferred to a later pass). Findings for next agent:
(1) Was blocked on bl-bdeb (verb dispatch run()->engine) — now CLOSED, so the forge variant's core mechanism (shell 'bl new --gates <parent>' at claim.post; 'bl close <gate-child>' at sync) is now runnable. Re-verify bl new/claim/close actually execute before relying on them.
(2) Ball bundles THREE separable deliverables, all in sibling repo /home/mark/dev/balls-github-plugin: (a) forge delivery variant — new-protocol plugin: approval gate child at claim.post, push work/<id>+PR at close.pre (NO local squash), forge sync closes gate on merge, rollback claim.post drops the gate child, rollback close.pre no-op, empty deliverable auto-closes its own gate child (§11 FORGE); (b) port github-issues from old clap CLI (push/sync --task + SyncReport on stdout) to §6/§7 subprocess protocol (<op> <phase> + wire on stdin, NO return channel — mutate worktree or shell bl); (c) §17 one-time legacy-adoption seeding plugins/github-issues/<id>/ XDG scratch from old external.github-issues.issue.number (zero-dup on next sync; skip-cost is 1 dup/task).
(3) No greenfield bl is installed (epic forbids make install until cutover) so sibling-repo integration tests can't exec a real bl — port plugins against fakes/seams like delivery's FakeRepo, or wait for cutover. Reference new-protocol plugins: src/bin/bl-delivery.rs + src/delivery.rs (dispatch matrix) + src/bin/tracker.rs.
(4) Consider splitting into sub-balls (forge / issues-port / §17-adoption) — likely too large for one ball.

author: Besting
text: AFFECTED by config/store split: the forge/delivery + github-issues plugins read the §7 binding, whose fields changed (operating → store/landing/tasks_branch — bl-f970); and their committed config crosses by install = path-copy (folder=mirror/glob=union, no object enum — bl-3dd9). The forge gate child + approval flow are unchanged. bl-2e26 §6/§7/§11.
